topics: Introduction to maritime law
Need and source of maritime law
Implementation of maritime law through national legislation
Role of United Nations (UN), International Maritime Organization (IMO) and International Labour Organization (ILO)
Role of flag state and port state in implementation of law
Introduction to SOLAS, MARPOL, STCW and Maritime Labour Convention (MLC)

Need of survey and certification, classification societies, United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) various zones and limits, Maritime Labour Convention: minimum requirements for seafarers to work on a ship, conditions of employment, accommodation, recreational facilities, food and catering, health protection, medical care, welfare and social security
SOLAS: Introduction, subdivision and stability, fire protection, detection and extinction, life saving appliances and arrangements, carriage of grain, carriage of dangerous goods
Load Line Convention: important definitions and markings, survey and certification
ISPS Code: ship security plan, security levels, declaration of security, ship security alert system, piracy, best management practices to deter piracy
Introduction to other conventions
International Convention on Salvage, Convention on Limitation of Liability for Maritime Claims
Oil Pollution Compensation Conventions
Shipping Management: Types of cargoes
Types of ships which carry them
Ship's tonnages (GT)
Cargo carrying capacity
Ship registration
Ship insurance
Ship owners, operators and managers
Types of shipping services: liner and tramp
Role of intermediaries in shipping business: freight brokers, clearing and forwarding agents, stevedores, shipbrokers, bunker and stores suppliers, shipping agencies
Shipping Economics: Sea transport and the global economy
Role of shipping in economic development
Shipping market cycles
Shipping risk
Shipping market supply and demand model
The four shipping markets: freight market, sale and purchase market, newbuilding market and demolition market
Classification of costs
Financing ships and shipping companies
Risk and returns
